title: Standardized container image for gofmt and lint
body:
Having a standardized image allows uniform application of format and
lint checking across multiple host platforms.  This ensures all
contributors and disparate CI systems to play by a common set of basic
rules.  It also makes it easier to maintain the common rules over-time.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Could you add some README to `./contrib/gate/` to describe the purpose of it?

For CI, the image should still be build locally to avoid a chicken-egg bootstrapping problem for PRs changing those scripts.

> Could you add some README to ./contrib/gate/ to describe the purpose of it?

Oh sure, good idea.  Should it have anything more than a link to ``CONTRIBUTING.md``?

> For CI, the image should still be build locally to avoid a chicken-egg bootstrapping problem for PRs changing those scripts.

Yes this is true, though hopefully a fairly rare event.  It would be possible to automate this, but I'll leave that as a learning exercise for someone else :D

> Oh sure, good idea. Should it have anything more than a link to CONTRIBUTING.md?

Just a short description plus the link sounds good to me, thanks a lot.

@cevich, thanks! LGTM

Can you squash the commits into a single one, so we can merge it?
@vrothberg done, and I added a lil more text to the readme.

@baude PTAL.  What I plan to do in `.cirrus.yml` is set the checkout directory to ``/usr/src/libpod`` instead of a volume-mount.   Can that work with `papr` and openshift?  If not, let me know what changes you'd like and I'll do 'em.
